screenshot_status: SUCCESS — desktop 1280x800 and mobile 375x812 captured via Puppeteer single-process mode after two initial failures (protocol timeout + pthread resource exhaustion on first two attempts). Both screenshots saved. Vibe at capture time: Crushing It, Monday Mar 9. All 5 workers visible on both viewports.

visual_observations.desktop: Dark theme intact, all 5 workers visible at desks. Bloberto at manager desk with CVO badge. REGRESSION FOUND: Bloberto's ambient speech bubble ('hour?! Legends. 🏆') is visibly CLIPPED at the top edge — only the lower two-thirds of the bubble are readable. The bubble uses `bottom: calc(100% + 8px)` which places it above the character, but Bloberto sits at top=4% inside an office-floor that has overflow:hidden, so the top of the bubble is guillotined. Ambient particles (7 pale dots) are rendering and drifting — subtle, not distracting. COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT badge in coffee corner. MIDNIGHT time caption on clock. Vibe weather ☀️ icon visible in header. Conference table lower-left, vacant job card partially visible at a desk.
visual_observations.mobile: All 5 workers visible with names below avatars (mobile fix still holding). Header shows vibe pill and weather icon correctly. Office floor scales down cleanly. Shortcuts bar wraps to three lines at 375px — very tight, borderline unusable on real small devices. Ambient particles visible as faint dots. Ficus rendering in lower-right area. Conference table present. Clock time caption not visible at mobile scale, expected.
visual_observations.regressions_vs_32: ONE NEW REGRESSION: Bloberto speech bubble clipped by office-floor overflow:hidden on desktop. This is a direct consequence of Bloberto being positioned at top={4} (Office.jsx line 1401) with the speech bubble extending above him — that top 4% gap is not enough headroom. Mobile shortcuts bar line-wrap is cosmetically unpleasant but functional.

fix_verification.settimeout_leak_ping: PARTIALLY FIXED ⚠️ — pingReactionTimerRef was added (Office.jsx line 1082) and handleCharClick now stores the ID in it (line 1095) and clears it before setting a new one (line 1094). The vibe useEffect cleanup at line 1139 also clears it: `() => { clearTimeout(t); clearTimeout(pingReactionTimerRef.current); }`. HOWEVER: that cleanup is inside the block guarded by the isFirstVibe.current early-return (line 1129). On initial mount, the vibe effect returns early with undefined — no cleanup is registered. If the component then unmounts with vibe never having changed (e.g., data never loads), pingReactionTimerRef.current fires on a dead component. Edge case, but the original bug report covered exactly this scenario. Needs a dedicated useEffect(() => () => clearTimeout(pingReactionTimerRef.current), []) to be truly safe.
fix_verification.vibe_weather_labels_key: FIXED ✅ — VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS (Office.jsx line 109) now correctly uses 'crushing' as the key, matching getTeamVibeKey() output. The vibe weather tooltip and aria-label will now properly show '☀️ Clear skies — team at full strength' instead of silently falling back to the bare vibe key string. About time.
fix_verification.ghost_char_focusable: NOT FIXED ❌ — Office.jsx line 644: tabIndex={0} is still unconditional on the Character wrapper. Ghost-variant characters still render with tabIndex={0}, no role, and no aria-label. Keyboard users tab into mute, unlabeled elements that do nothing on activation.
fix_verification.stuck_card_aria_label: NOT FIXED ❌ — App.jsx lines 458-481: stat-card--stuck still has no aria-label. The element has role='button' and tabIndex={0} (good from #32) but the inner text is '⚠️ Stuck' which most screen readers will announce as 'warning sign Stuck'. Worker name is inside a span with no aria labeling context.

score_notes: Visual drops from 4 to 3 — a new regression where Bloberto's speech bubble is clipped is hard to ignore. The boss literally cannot speak. UX holds at 3 with two medium a11y carry-overs still open. Code quality stays at 2: Office.jsx grew by 2 lines to 1515, FULL_IDLE_MESSAGES/IDLE_BUBBLE_EMOJIS/ERROR_BUBBLE_MESSAGES are still allocated inside component render bodies, and the three fragmented @media 480px blocks and duplicate unquoted CSS selectors remain. The VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS fix is appreciated — that was embarrassingly overdue. Fun factor is a hard 5: COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT is inspired.

suggestions.title: NEW: Bloberto speech bubble clipped by office-floor overflow:hidden
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1401 places Bloberto at top={4} inside the office-floor which has overflow:hidden (Office.css line 84). The .speech-bubble is positioned at `bottom: calc(100% + 8px)` above the character, putting its top edge at roughly -1% of the container — it gets sliced off. In the live desktop screenshot only the lower portion of the speech bubble text is visible. Fix options: (1) Increase Bloberto's top offset from 4 to at least 12 (simple, one-character change), or (2) add a CSS exception `.char--manager .speech-bubble { bottom: auto; top: calc(100% + 8px); }` to flip the bubble below Bloberto when he's near the top edge. Option 1 is simpler but may affect layout. Option 2 is surgical and safe.

suggestions.title: [6+ REVIEWS] setPingReaction setTimeout still leaks on vibe-stable unmount
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1082: pingReactionTimerRef now exists and is used correctly in handleCharClick (progress!). The vibe useEffect cleanup at line 1139 clears it on vibe changes and on unmount — IF the vibe ever changed. The critical gap: on initial mount, the vibe useEffect hits the isFirstVibe.current early-return (line 1129) and registers NO cleanup function (returns undefined). If the component mounts, a user pings someone (setting pingReactionTimerRef.current), and then Office unmounts before any vibe change occurs, the 3-second timer fires on the dead component. Fix requires adding one useEffect: `useEffect(() => () => clearTimeout(pingReactionTimerRef.current), [])` directly after pingReactionTimerRef is declared. Four lines. This has been open for 6+ reviews.

suggestions.title: [3 REVIEWS] Ghost (OOO) character is focusable with no role or aria-label
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 644: tabIndex={0} is applied unconditionally to every Character wrapper. Ghost-variant characters have no onClick handler, so they also have no role='button' and no aria-label. Keyboard users tab onto a silent, unidentifiable element. The ghost-ooo-bubble content (worker name, role, last-seen) is never surfaced to AT. Fix: when variant === 'ghost', either apply tabIndex={-1} to remove it from tab order, or add aria-label={`${worker.name} — out of office, ${worker.role}`} so it's at least identifiable. The ghost-ooo-bubble already has the right content; it just needs to be wired up to aria.

suggestions.title: [3 REVIEWS] stat-card--stuck missing aria-label — emoji read literally
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 458-481: the stuck worker card now has role='button' and onKeyDown (from review #31 fixes), but still no aria-label. A screen reader announces '⚠️ Stuck' — most ATs will say 'warning sign Stuck' followed by the worker info. Add aria-label={`Stuck: ${stuckWorkers.length === 1 ? `${stuckWorkers[0].name}, click to scroll to card` : `${stuckWorkers.length} workers stuck, click to scroll to first`}`}. This is a one-liner that unblocks full keyboard + screen reader accessibility for this card. It has been open for 3 reviews.

suggestions.title: [3 REVIEWS] char-hover-card overflows viewport on rightmost desk characters
suggestions.description: Office.css: .char-hover-card uses `position: absolute; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); bottom: 110%`. Characters assigned to the rightmost desks (Sofia at ~57% left, Carlos at ~66%) will have their hover cards extend past the right edge of the office-floor container. The card will be clipped by overflow:hidden on the container. Fix: add `.char--rightmost .char-hover-card { left: auto; right: 0; transform: none; }` or pass a data-side='right' attribute to Characters at DESKS with left > 50%, then target `.char[data-side='right'] .char-hover-card` in CSS. Code change affects Office.jsx desk-character rendering and one CSS rule.

suggestions.title: [3 REVIEWS] Duplicate unquoted .office-floor[data-vibe=...] rules in Office.css
suggestions.description: Office.css lines 91-95 declare .office-floor[data-vibe=crushing] (unquoted) with only border-color and box-shadow. Lines 97-114 redeclare the exact same selectors WITH quotes, also adding --grid-line custom properties. The unquoted set is functionally redundant and creates specificity noise. Just delete lines 91-95. The quoted rules at 97-114 are the complete, correct set. This has been flagged three reviews running.

suggestions.title: [3 REVIEWS] Hoist FULL_IDLE_MESSAGES, IDLE_BUBBLE_EMOJIS, ERROR_BUBBLE_MESSAGES out of render
suggestions.description: Three static objects are still recreated on every render: FULL_IDLE_MESSAGES inside Office() at line 1064, IDLE_BUBBLE_EMOJIS inside Character() at line 482, ERROR_BUBBLE_MESSAGES inside Character() at line 498. None of these depend on props or state. Moving them to module scope above the component functions eliminates allocations on every render cycle. With a 30s polling interval triggering re-renders and multiple Character instances, this is accumulated waste. Three cut-and-paste operations, zero logic changes.

suggestions.title: [3 REVIEWS] Three fragmented @media (max-width: 480px) blocks with duplicate rules
suggestions.description: Office.css still has three separate @media (max-width: 480px) blocks. Duplicate rule declarations: .office-wrap appears in two, .char__name in two, .wb-vibe-text in three. One block still carries the misleading comment '/* === Mobile Office Scale Fix (Luna Review #41) === */' — Luna is a character not a reviewer, and review #41 hasn't happened yet (we're on #33). Merge all three blocks into one at the end of Office.css, deduplicate rules, delete the nonsense comment. Prevents future devs from wondering why their mobile CSS change has no effect because it's being overridden three blocks later.

suggestions.title: [3 REVIEWS] Office.jsx at 1,515 lines and still growing
suggestions.description: Office.jsx grew by 2 more lines since review #32 (now 1515). CharacterAvatar, Character, AwaySign, WindowElement, ConferenceTable, VibeShockwave, WallClock, and Office are all in one file. The ambient-particles feature was added inline rather than extracted. Suggested split: src/office/CharacterAvatar.jsx, src/office/Character.jsx, src/office/OfficeFurniture.jsx (Window, Table, Clock, VibeShockwave), src/office/Office.jsx (orchestrator, ~300 lines). Every new PR on this file requires loading the entire 1500-line context to find one component. This is not getting better on its own.

suggestions.title: NEW: [...Array(7)] allocated on every render in ambient-particles
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1502: `{[...Array(7)].map((_, i) => ...)}` creates a new array on every render. Since the particle count and positions are static, this should either be extracted as a module-level constant `const PARTICLE_COUNT = 7` and rendered with `Array.from({length: PARTICLE_COUNT})`, or better yet, the entire particle JSX array can be memoized with useMemo (no deps) or computed once at module scope. Minor perf item but easy to fix while in the file for other changes.

suggestions.title: Ficus plant has aria-hidden='true' but carries a meaningful title tooltip
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 1452-1462: the ficus div has aria-hidden='true' AND title={`Streak: ${vibeStreak} · ${plantStageLabel} ${plantStageEmoji[plantStage]}`}. The aria-hidden prevents AT from discovering the element at all, which also suppresses the title tooltip for screen reader users. The ficus streak is a meaningful data point (it reflects team health over time). Either remove aria-hidden and add a proper aria-label with the streak text, or keep aria-hidden but also expose the streak data in the stats bar or an sr-only element so it's actually discoverable.

suggestions.title: lone-survivor-label still uses 7 inline styles — create a CSS class
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1419: the lone-survivor-label element still carries `style={{ fontSize: '0.45rem', opacity: 0.6, position: 'absolute', bottom: '-0.6rem', left: '50%', transform: 'translateX(-50%)', whiteSpace: 'nowrap', color: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.7)' }}`. Seven inline style properties cannot be targeted by media queries, prefers-reduced-motion, or theme overrides. Add .lone-survivor-label to Office.css and remove the style prop. Was flagged in review #32, still not done.

suggestions.title: Move VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS and VIBE_WEATHER_ICONS to utils/constants.js
suggestions.description: These two lookup objects (Office.jsx lines 108-121) are pure static data with no component dependencies. They belong in src/utils/constants.js alongside ROLE_COLORS, VIBE_WHITEBOARD etc. Moving them would reduce Office.jsx line count by 14 lines and put all vibe-keyed data in one auditable location — which is exactly where the 'crushing-it' vs 'crushing' key mismatch would have been caught in review #31 instead of review #32. Now that the key is fixed, lock it down by co-locating it with getTeamVibeKey().
